chunk_summary: Antenatal lactation counseling identifies risk factors, sets postpartum plans, and provides anticipatory skills training to optimize breastfeeding outcomes.
learning_objectives: Identify antenatal risk factors for delayed lactogenesis or low milk production.
Develop postpartum plans emphasizing skin-to-skin contact, early breastfeeding, and rooming-in.
Provide anticipatory guidance on latch techniques, colostrum harvesting, and pain management.
key_facts: Screen for risk factors such as prior breast surgery, diabetes, hypothyroidism, obesity, and minimal breast growth during pregnancy.
Plan early hand expression within 1–2 hours after birth and aim for 8–10 milk removals per 24 hours if direct feeding is delayed.
Normalize colostrum volumes and frequent early feeds to prevent prelacteals and unnecessary formula use.
Antenatal colostrum harvesting may be considered for mothers at risk of early separation, with obstetric approval.
Persistent nipple pain is abnormal; safe analgesia options should be discussed postpartum.
reference_quote: Antenatal counseling sets realistic expectations and identifies dyads likely to need early lactation support. (Source: https://abm.memberclicks.net/assets/DOCUMENTS/PROTOCOLS/19prenatal%20promotionpro...)
document_key_topics: Early milk expression and colostrum harvesting
